label: Craft Name
description: The name of your quad as displayed in the OSD and Blackbox logs. No spaces â€” use underscores.

label: Bidirectional DSHOT
description: Enables RPM telemetry from ESCs for RPM-based filtering. Requires ESC firmware with bidir support (BLHeli_32, AM32, Bluejay).
note: Changing this requires matching ESC firmware support â€” edit manually if needed.

label: DSHOT Idle Value
description: Idle throttle signal sent to ESCs when armed but not flying. Higher values spin motors faster at idle. Typical range: 550â€“1100.

label: Motor Poles
description: Number of magnetic poles in your motors. Critical for accurate RPM filtering. Most micro motors = 12 poles; larger 5" motors often = 14.

label: Dynamic Idle Min RPM
description: Minimum RPM the FC will target during idle to keep motors spinning. Prevents stalls on fast descents. Set to 0 to disable.

label: Motor Output Limit (%)
description: Caps maximum motor output as a percentage. Useful for reducing power on overpowered builds or indoor flying.

label: Small Angle Limit (Â°)
description: Maximum tilt angle allowed for arming. Default 25Â° prevents arming when tilted. Set to 180 to arm at any angle.

label: FPV Camera Angle (Â°)
description: Tilt angle of your FPV camera. Used with FPV Angle Mix so the horizon stays level in your feed during forward flight.

label: Throttle Limit Type
description: How throttle limiting is applied. SCALE compresses the full range into the limit. CLIP hard-cuts at the limit value.

label: Throttle Limit (%)
description: Maximum throttle allowed as a percentage. Only active when Throttle Limit Type is not OFF.

label: Failsafe Delay (Ã— 0.1s)
description: Time with no RC signal before failsafe activates, in tenths of a second. Default 4 = 0.4s.

label: Failsafe Recovery Delay (Ã— 0.1s)
description: Time signal must be re-established before exiting failsafe. Default 20 = 2.0s.

label: Beacon Motor Tone
description: Which motor beeper tone plays when using DSHOT beacon (find-my-quad). Values 1â€“5, each with a different pitch/rhythm.

label: Max Cell Voltage (mV)
description: Maximum voltage per cell in millivolts. Standard LiPo = 420mV. HV LiPo = 430â€“450mV. Used to calculate battery percentage.

label: Min Cell Voltage (mV)
description: Minimum safe voltage per cell. Going below this damages cells. Typical: 320â€“330mV for standard LiPo.

label: Warning Cell Voltage (mV)
description: Voltage per cell that triggers the low-battery OSD warning. Set ~10â€“20mV above your minimum.

label: Battery Capacity (mAh)
description: Your battery capacity in milliamp-hours. Used to calculate remaining charge and flight time in the OSD.

label: Voltage Sag Compensation (%)
description: Automatically boosts motor output to compensate for voltage drop under load. 100% = full compensation. Helps the quad feel consistent as battery drains.

label: Battery Voltage Scale
description: Voltage sensor calibration â€” unique to each FC unit. Must be verified with a multimeter. Never copy between builds.

label: Current Sensor Scale
description: Current sensor calibration â€” unique to each FC unit. Calibrate against a known current draw. Never copy between builds.

label: Roll P
description: Proportional gain for roll. Controls how strongly the FC corrects roll errors. Too high = oscillations (vibration at steady throttle). Too low = mushy, unresponsive roll.

label: Roll I
description: Integral gain for roll. Corrects sustained errors and helps hold angle against wind. Too high = slow wobble or I-windup (delayed oscillation).

label: Roll D
description: Derivative gain for roll. Dampens overshoot and prop wash. Too high = hot motors and high-frequency buzzing oscillations.

label: Roll F (Feedforward)
description: Feedforward for roll. Adds anticipation to stick inputs for a snappier, more responsive feel. Too high = overshoots on fast stick flicks.

label: Roll D Min
description: Minimum D for roll when idle. D scales dynamically between D-Min (at rest) and D (at full stick). Reduces motor heat during hover.

label: Roll D Max
description: Maximum D for roll during fast stick inputs. D-Max kicks in during quick manoeuvres to dampen overshoot.

label: Pitch P
description: Proportional gain for pitch. Controls pitch correction strength. Typically slightly higher than roll P on most quads.

label: Pitch I
description: Integral gain for pitch. Corrects sustained pitch errors. Helps hold angle in forward flight.

label: Pitch D
description: Derivative gain for pitch. Dampens pitch oscillations and prop wash.

label: Pitch F (Feedforward)
description: Feedforward for pitch. Adds anticipation to pitch stick inputs.

label: Pitch D Min
description: Minimum D for pitch when idle. Scales up during fast pitch inputs.

label: Pitch D Max
description: Maximum D for pitch during fast stick inputs.

label: Yaw P
description: Proportional gain for yaw. Controls yaw correction strength. Yaw typically runs lower P than roll/pitch due to propeller gyroscopic effects.

label: Yaw I
description: Integral gain for yaw. Helps hold heading. Usually matched to roll I.

label: Yaw F (Feedforward)
description: Feedforward for yaw. Adds anticipation to yaw stick inputs.

label: D Max Gain
description: Controls how quickly D scales from D-Min to D-Max during stick inputs. Higher = faster transition. Applied to all axes.

label: I-Term Limit
description: Caps maximum I-term accumulation to prevent I-windup â€” where I keeps building when the quad can't respond fast enough.

label: I-Term Relax Cutoff (Hz)
description: Reduces I-term during fast stick moves (flips/rolls) to prevent bounce-back. Lower cutoff = more relaxation.

label: Throttle Boost
description: Boosts throttle response on fast inputs. Helps with prop wash on fast descents. Too high = bouncy, unpredictable throttle.

label: Thrust Linearisation (%)
description: Compensates for the non-linear thrust curve of propellers. Helps PIDs feel consistent across the throttle range. Typical: 20â€“40%.

label: Feedforward Jitter Factor
description: Smooths feedforward to reduce twitchiness from RC signal noise. Higher = more smoothing, less jitter amplification.

label: Yaw PID Sum Limit
description: Caps the total yaw PID output. Prevents yaw from overpowering roll/pitch corrections during combined manoeuvres.

label: Master Multiplier (%)
description: Scales all PIDs up or down proportionally. Quick way to make the tune more or less aggressive without touching individual values.

label: PI Gain (%)
description: Scales both P and I gains together relative to the master multiplier.

label: I Gain (%)
description: Scales all I-term values independently.

label: D Gain (%)
description: Scales all D-term values independently.

label: Pitch PI Gain (%)
description: Scales pitch P and I independently from roll. Useful if pitch/roll response feels unbalanced.

label: Pitch D Gain (%)
description: Scales pitch D independently from roll D.

label: Feedforward Gain (%)
description: Scales all feedforward values relative to the master multiplier.

label: D Max Gain (%)
description: Scales the D-Max boost range (how much D increases during fast inputs).

label: D Max Gain (%) [legacy]
description: Legacy name for D Max Gain on older Betaflight versions. Same function as simplified_d_max_gain.

label: Gyro Filter Multiplier (%)
description: Scales all gyro filter cutoff frequencies together. Higher = less filtering (sharper response, more noise). Lower = more filtering (smoother, more phase delay).

label: D-Term Filter Multiplier (%)
description: Scales D-term filter cutoff frequencies. Higher = less D-term filtering. Lower = more filtering but slower D-term response.

label: Gyro LPF1 Static (Hz)
description: First gyro low-pass filter cutoff frequency. Set to 0 to use dynamic mode. Lower = more noise rejection, more phase delay.

label: Gyro LPF1 Dynamic Min (Hz)
description: Minimum cutoff for dynamic gyro LPF1. Filter won't go below this frequency.

label: Gyro LPF1 Dynamic Max (Hz)
description: Maximum cutoff for dynamic gyro LPF1. Filter won't go above this frequency.

label: Gyro LPF2 Static (Hz)
description: Second gyro low-pass filter cutoff. Adds an extra noise rejection stage after LPF1. Set to 0 to disable.

label: Gyro LPF2 Type
description: Filter shape for gyro LPF2. PT1 = gentle rolloff. PT2/PT3 = steeper rolloff with more phase delay. BIQUAD = steepest.

label: D-Term LPF1 Static (Hz)
description: First D-term low-pass filter cutoff. D-term is inherently noisy â€” this is its primary noise filter. Set to 0 for dynamic mode.

label: D-Term LPF1 Dynamic Min (Hz)
description: Minimum cutoff for dynamic D-term LPF1.

label: D-Term LPF1 Dynamic Max (Hz)
description: Maximum cutoff for dynamic D-term LPF1.

label: D-Term LPF1 Dynamic Expo
description: Shapes the dynamic D-term filter curve. Higher = filter spends more time at lower frequencies (more filtering at rest).

label: D-Term LPF2 Static (Hz)
description: Second D-term low-pass filter. Additional noise rejection after LPF1.

label: Dynamic Notch Count
description: Number of dynamic notch filters active simultaneously (0â€“4). More notches = better noise targeting but added phase delay.

label: Dynamic Notch Min (Hz)
description: Lowest frequency the dynamic notch will track. Set based on your lowest expected motor noise (low RPM noise floor).

label: Dynamic Notch Max (Hz)
description: Highest frequency the dynamic notch will track.

label: Dynamic Notch Q Factor
description: Bandwidth of each notch filter. Lower Q = wider notch (catches more noise, removes more signal). Higher Q = narrower, more precise.

label: RPM Filter Weights (1st,2nd,3rd)
description: Strength of RPM notch filters for the 1st, 2nd, and 3rd motor harmonics. Format: 100,20,100. Set all to 0 to disable. Requires bidirectional DSHOT.

label: RPM Filter Fade Range (Hz)
description: Frequency range over which RPM notch filters fade in from 0 Hz. Prevents targeting very low RPM noise at idle.

label: Yaw Lowpass (Hz)
description: Low-pass filter on yaw PID output. Reduces yaw noise and twitchiness. Set to 0 to disable.

label: RC Deadband
description: Stick deadband around centre for roll and pitch (in Âµs). Inputs within this range are treated as centred. Helps with drifty or slightly off-centre sticks.

label: Yaw Deadband
description: Stick deadband around centre for the yaw axis. Same concept as RC Deadband but for yaw only.

label: ELRS Model ID
description: Model Match ID for ExpressLRS. When Model Match is enabled on your TX, the RX only pairs with a TX sending this ID. Set to 0 to disable Model Match.

label: ELRS Packet Rate
description: Default RF packet rate index for ELRS SPI receivers. Lower index = higher frequency = lower latency but shorter range.

label: VTX Band
description: Video transmitter frequency band. 1=Boscam A, 2=Boscam B, 3=Boscam E, 4=Fatshark/Airwave, 5=Race Band. Check your goggles for supported bands.

label: VTX Channel
description: Video transmitter channel within the selected band (1â€“8). Coordinate with other pilots to avoid frequency clashes.

label: VTX Power Level
description: Transmit power index. Specific power levels (mW) depend on your VTX model. 1 = lowest power. Always fly at the lowest power that gives you reliable video.

label: VTX Frequency (MHz)
description: Output frequency derived from your band and channel selection. Read-only reference.

label: Rate Profile Name
description: Display name for this rate profile, shown in the OSD rate profile indicator.

label: Rates Type
description: Rate calculation method. BETAFLIGHT = classic RC Rate/Super Rate/Expo system. ACTUAL = set max deg/s directly. QUICK = simplified curve.

label: Roll RC Rate
description: Base roll rate multiplier. Controls overall roll speed. Higher = faster rolls for same stick input.

label: Roll Super Rate
description: Exponential roll rate boost at full stick. 0 = linear. Higher = faster at full deflection, slower near centre.

label: Roll Expo
description: Softens roll stick feel around centre. Higher = more sluggish centre feel, better precision for filming. 0 = no expo.

label: Pitch RC Rate
description: Base pitch rate. Usually matched to Roll RC Rate.

label: Pitch Super Rate
description: Exponential pitch rate boost at full stick.

label: Pitch Expo
description: Softens pitch feel around centre.

label: Yaw RC Rate
description: Base yaw rate. Usually set lower than roll/pitch for more controlled yaw inputs.

label: Yaw Super Rate
description: Exponential yaw rate boost at full stick.

label: Yaw Expo
description: Softens yaw feel around centre.

label: Hover Throttle Mid (%)
description: BF 4.2+ only: internal hover throttle midpoint reference used by altitude-aware modes (EZ Landing, turtle mode). Does NOT affect regular throttle stick feel or response curve in acro/freestyle flying.

label: Hover Throttle (%)
description: Estimated throttle percentage at which your quad hovers. Used by EZ Landing, turtle mode recovery, and similar altitude-aware features to determine a throttle baseline. Has no effect in pure acro flying.
